title: Variations in populations
description: Test your knowledge of variations in populations!

assessment_items.question_content:
A scientist is studying the relationship between genetic diversity and population resilience. The researcher defines **resilience** as the percentage of a population that survives over a certain time period after the onset of environmental change.

**Which of the following graphs best represents the relationship between the genetic diversity within a population and the population’s resilience?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Populations with more genetic diversity contain a larger number of alleles, and therefore a larger range of phenotypes.

This means that populations with more genetic diversity are more resilient to environmental changes, because they are more likely to contain individuals who have the phenotypes needed to withstand the new environmental pressure.
So, the correct graph will show population resilience increasing as genetic diversity increases.

choices.content: ![The y axis of the line graph is labeled Population resilience and the x axis is labeled Genetic diversity. The line on the graph begins at the origin of the x axis and near the start of the y axis and is a solid diagonal line that extends upwards and towards the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8639034d477629223adf4ce4b18aac71938e6310)

![The y axis of the line graph is labeled Population resilience and the x axis is labeled Genetic diversity. The line on the graph begins at the origin of the x axis and the top of the y axis and is a solid diagonal line that extends down and towards the right](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b3747ed636213af72975792e0b8ffae17d408e23)

![The y axis of the line graph is labeled Population resilience and the x axis is labeled Genetic diversity. The line on the graph begins at the origin of the x axis and the top of the y axis, and is a solid line that descends rapidly at the beginning and then levels off at the mid-point of the x axis and ends near the bottom of the y axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4fe9dab8768eebfe04a03553a89a5c20f35003a5)

![The y axis of the line graph is labeled Population resilience and the x axis is labeled Genetic diversity. The line on the graph begins at the origin of the x axis and y axis and is a bell-shaped curve that peaks in the center of the x axis and the top of the y axis and the line ends at the end of the x axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1cf63f3df73b8f27a0bc2cdec364fde0e008a947)

assessment_items.question_content:
A scientist is studying the relationship between genetic diversity and a population’s mortality rate. The scientist defines the **mortality rate** as the percentage of the population that dies over a certain time period following an environmental disturbance.

**Which of the following graphs best represents the relationship between the genetic diversity within a population and the population’s mortality rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Populations with more genetic diversity contain a larger number of alleles, and therefore a larger range of phenotypes.

This means that populations with more genetic diversity are likely to have a lower mortality rate in response to an environmental disturbance, because they are more likely to contain individuals who have the phenotypes needed to survive the environmental stress.
So, the correct graph will show the mortality rate decreasing as genetic diversity increases.

choices.content: ![The y axis of the line graph is labeled Mortality rate and the x axis is labeled Genetic diversity. The line on the graph begins at the origin of the x axis and the top of the y axis and is a solid diagonal line that extends down and towards the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce8dd446e65fc5dd8046194c2856a6fbfc0867fd)

![The y axis of the line graph is labeled Mortality rate and the x axis is labeled Genetic diversity. The line on the graph begins at the origin of the x axis and near the start of the y axis and is a solid diagonal line that extends upwards and towards the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7540fd12d6f8e9edfd87e4e80e6557b0a490ccd1)

![The y axis of the line graph is labeled Mortality rate and the x axis is labeled Genetic diversity. The line on the graph begins at the origin of the x axis and y axis and is a slightly curved solid line extending upward and to the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/499234861ccb90c7deffcf015ce1db0d93f50135)

![The y axis of the line graph is labeled Mortality rate and the x axis is labeled Genetic diversity. The line on the graph begins at the origin of the x axis and y axis and is a bell shaped curve that peaks in the center of the x axis and the top of the y axis, and the line ends at the end of the x axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f3fb86dbab50d57fd96f2fd1d0e56911a58bc53)

assessment_items.question_content:
A scientist is studying the relationship between genetic diversity and population resilience. The researcher defines **resilience** as the percentage of a population that survives over a certain time period after the onset of environmental change.

**Which of the following statements best expresses the relationship between a population’s genetic diversity and its resilience?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Populations with more genetic diversity contain a larger number of alleles, and therefore a larger range of phenotypes.

This means that populations with more genetic diversity are more resilient to environmental changes, because they are more likely to contain individuals who have the phenotypes needed to withstand the new environmental pressure.

So, the correct answer is:

* As genetic diversity increases, so does resilience.

choices.content: As genetic diversity increases, so does resilience.
As genetic diversity decreases, resilience remains about the same.
Resilience is highest at low levels of genetic diversity.
Resilience is lowest at moderate levels of genetic diversity.

assessment_items.question_content:
A population of potato plants is growing in a farmer’s field. The potatoes were propagated vegetatively, so they are all genetic clones of each other. Nearby, a population of wild potato plants is growing on a hillside. Plants in this population reproduce primarily through sexual reproduction. Both potato plant populations have similar population densities. 

A fungus-like, oomycete pathogen is introduced to the area and infects both populations at roughly the same time.

**Which of the following is the best prediction for how the oomycete pathogen will affect the two potato plant populations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The hillside population will be less severely affected because it is more likely to have individuals with some level of resistance to the pathogen.
The field population will be less severely affected because it has less allelic diversity than the hillside population.
The field population will be more severely affected because it has more individuals planted closer together than the hillside population.
The two populations will be equally affected because they were infected nearly simultaneously.

assessment_items.question_content:
A group of researchers grows two populations of rice in separate, equal-sized fields. Population 1 is made up of multiple varieties of rice, each variety having a unique set of traits. Population 2 is made up of a single variety. Both rice plant populations have similar population densities.

The researchers introduce a fungal pathogen to both populations, making sure to apply roughly equal numbers of spores to each field.

**Which of the following is the best prediction for how the fungal pathogen will affect the two rice populations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Population 2 will be more severely affected because it has less allelic diversity than Population 1.
Population 2 will be less severely affected because it is more likely to contain individuals with some level of resistance to the pathogen.
Population 1 will be more severely affected because it contains more individuals than Population 2 does.
The two populations will be equally affected because they were infected with roughly equal numbers of spores.

assessment_items.question_content:
A population of banana plants is growing in an orchard. The banana plants were propagated vegetatively, so they are all genetic clones of each other. Nearby, a population of wild banana plants is growing in a jungle habitat. Plants in this population reproduce primarily through sexual reproduction. Both banana plant populations have similar population densities. 

A fungal pathogen is introduced to the area and infects both populations at roughly the same time.

**Which of the following is the best prediction for how the fungal pathogen will affect the two banana plant populations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The orchard population will be more severely affected because it has less allelic diversity than the jungle population.
The orchard population will be less severely affected because it is more likely to have individuals with some level of resistance to the pathogen.
The jungle population will be more severely affected because it is growing near other species of plants in the jungle habitat.
The two populations will be equally affected because they were infected nearly simultaneously.

assessment_items.question_content:
California condors are large, North American vultures that nearly went extinct in the 1980s as a result of habitat destruction. Through captive breeding and reintroduction of populations into the wild, the number of wild California condors is slowly increasing. However, because of the population bottleneck, all of the condor populations that currently exist in the wild are descended from a breeding population of just 14 individuals.

**Which two claims are best supported by the information provided above?**

👁️ **Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Wild California condor populations have little genetic diversity and are therefore at risk of decline.
Because of the bottleneck, the wild California condor populations are less likely to contain individuals that can withstand a certain environmental change.
Wild California condor populations can better withstand disruptions to their habitats than they could before the population bottleneck.
Because of the bottleneck, the wild California condor populations are more resilient, containing individuals whose genes were selected for by natural selection.

assessment_items.question_content:
The black-footed ferret is a species of North American mammal that nearly went extinct in the 1980s as a result of habitat destruction and disease. Through captive breeding and reintroduction of populations into their natural habitats, the number of wild black-footed ferrets is slowly increasing. However, because of the population bottleneck, all of the black-footed ferrets that currently exist in the wild are descended from a breeding population of just seven individuals.

**Which two claims are best supported by the information provided above?**

👁️ **Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Wild black-footed ferret populations have little genetic diversity and are therefore still at risk of extinction.
Because of the bottleneck, the wild black-footed ferret populations are less likely to contain individuals that can withstand changes to their habitats.
Wild black-footed ferret populations can better withstand an outbreak of disease than they could before the population bottleneck.
Because of the bottleneck, the wild black-footed ferret populations are better adapted to their environment, containing individuals whose genes were selected for by natural selection.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the 1800’s, there were millions of greater prairie chickens in the state of Illinois. However, by 1993 there were fewer than 50 as a result of hunting and the conversion of prairie habitats to farmland. Conservation efforts have helped to increase the number of greater prairie chickens in Illinois, but their numbers are still small.

**Which two claims are best supported by the information provided above?**

👁️ **Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Greater prairie chicken populations in Illinois have little genetic diversity and are therefore at risk of decline.
Because of the bottleneck, the greater prairie chicken populations in Illinois are less likely to contain individuals that can withstand a certain environmental pressure.
The greater prairie chicken populations in Illinois can better withstand exposure to agricultural pollution than they could before the population bottleneck.
Because of the bottleneck, the greater prairie chicken population in Illinois is better adapted to live on farmland, containing individuals whose genes were selected for by natural selection.

assessment_items.question_content:
Destruction of swamplands has reduced the area inhabited by a certain panther population. As a result, the panther population is small, highly inbred, and at risk of extinction.

**Which of the following actions can humans take to decrease the panther population’s risk of extinction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Introduce a small number of panthers from a genetically distinct population into the at-risk population.
Move half of the population to a different, isolated swampland habitat where there is little competition for resources.
Add other species of large cat to the area to increase species diversity in the swampland habitat.
Use the fittest male and female from the population in a captive breeding program that will increase the population’s size.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Ozark mountains contain habitats called glades, which are hot, open patches of land surrounded by forest. Glades are home to many animal species, including the collared lizard.

In a certain region, scientists have found that the collared lizard populations in neighboring glades are small and lacking in genetic diversity, which puts them at risk of extinction. The populations are isolated from each other by thick forest undergrowth. Historically, this undergrowth was removed by periodic wildfires, but humans have been actively preventing wildfires in the area.

**Which of the following actions can humans take to decrease the risk of extinction for the collared lizard populations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Use controlled fires to remove forest undergrowth between glades to promote interbreeding between populations.
Allow wildfires to burn portions of the forested areas to make individual glades larger, which would provide more resources for collared lizards.
Introduce other lizard species to the glades to increase species diversity in the collared lizard’s habitat.
Reduce the size of each collared lizard population by half to decrease competition for resources in each glade.

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers have found that elk populations in a certain national park are small and lacking in genetic diversity, which puts them at risk of extinction. These populations are isolated from each other by busy highways that cut through the national park’s land.

**Which of the following actions can humans take to decrease the risk of extinction for the elk populations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Build walkways for the elks over highways to promote interbreeding among the populations.
Reduce the size of each elk population by one-fourth in order to decrease competition for resources.
Introduce new mammal species to the national park to increase species diversity in the elk’s habitat.
Use the fittest male and female from one elk population in a captive breeding program to increase the number of elk in the national park.
